text: Fill in the blanks about the lymphatic system.
questions: The *thoracic* duct drains lymph from the entire body except the right side of the head, neck, and right upper limb, which drain via the *right lymphatic* duct.
Approximately *3:in litres* litres of fluid per day leaks from blood capillaries into the interstitium and must be returned by the lymphatic system.
Specialised lymphatic capillaries in intestinal villi called *lacteals* absorb dietary fats as *chylomicrons*.
Lymphatic capillaries have overlapping endothelial cells with no tight junctions and a *discontinuous/absent* basement membrane, unlike blood capillaries.
Secondary lymphoedema in coastal Tamil Nadu is commonly caused by *Wuchereria bancrofti* infection, leading to elephantiasis.

overallFeedback.feedback: Keep studying — review the material and try again.
